title: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Troy University)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Alabama Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Greetings,

I am looking to make a request for records related to your athletic department.

1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined.

2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams.

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time.

Best,

Greg Chick

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

André Natta, a citizen of Alabama, in coordination with Greg Chick.

title: SLP PD Flock Safety Audit

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: SLP PD Flock Safety Audit
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) ALPR Audit Trail Logs (All Access & Activity)
Records comprising the ALPR data audit trail documenting all queries, searches, alerts, access, sharing, or dissemination events from 1/1/2025 through the date this request is processed. Per department policy, all such actions must be recorded in a data audit trail. Please include all available metadata, including but not limited to:
- Date and time
- User identifier (name or role)
- Query/search parameters
- Action taken (search, alert review, data access, sharing, etc.)

Please provide these records in a structured format (e.g., CSV, XLSX, or similar database format).
------
2) Access Justification / Purpose Logs
Records documenting the purpose, justification, or case association for each ALPR query or access event during the same time period. Department policy restricts access to legitimate law enforcement purposes tied to investigations or official actions. 
------
3) External Access / Inter-Agency Query Logs
Records of any access to SLP PD ALPR data by external agencies, including:
- Requesting agency
- Requestor identity (or role)
- Date/time
- Stated purpose
- Associated case number (if available)

Department policy requires written requests and documented justification for such access.
[Note - Common Flock usage logs may group 1, 2, and 3 together into one excel file. That is hopefully less work for you, and easier for us.] 
------
4) Manual Hot List Entries and Justifications
Records of all Manual Hot List entries during the requested time period, including:
- Plate entered
- Date of entry/removal
- Justification or associated investigation

Policy requires a “legitimate and specific law enforcement reason” for such entries.
------
5) Agreements with Private Entities (Flock / ALPR Access)
Any agreements, memoranda, or contracts with:

- Homeowner associations
- Private businesses
- Individuals
- Religious entities or groups

Relating to ALPR or Flock system deployment, access, or data sharing.
------
6) Electronic Communications with Flock Safety
Correspondence (emails, letters, memoranda) between SLP PD personnel and Flock Safety (domain: @flocksafety.com). Timeframe: 6/1/2025 to present
You may de-duplicate mass emails or newsletters.
------
7) Biennial ALPR Audit Reports and Submissions
Copies of all biennial ALPR audit reports, including any reports, summaries, or submissions provided to the Minnesota Commissioner of Administration, legislative committees, or other oversight bodies, for the past two years.
------

I realize this is a large request and appreciate you taking the time to complete it. Please feel free to respond in multiple parts as documents become available.

I have already reviewed: 
A)  Flock Transparency Page: https://transparency.flocksafety.com/saint-louis-park-mn-pd
B) SLP PD ALPR Policy: https://www.stlouisparkmn.gov/home/showpublisheddocument/28047/638681458304130000
C) Location of Devices: https://dps.mn.gov/divisions/bca/data-and-reports/agencies-use-lprs-lpr

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

title: Robert Mueller

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Robert Mueller
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records mentioning, relating to, or generated as a result of investigation or inquiry into former FBI Director Robert Swan Mueller III (August 7, 1944 – March 20, 2026). His death is widely-known and widely reported; as such, privacy concerns under FOIA Exemptions 6 and 7 are voided, as are any concerns under the Privacy Act. For example, you may find his New York Times obituary at the following link: https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/21/us/politics/robert-s-mueller-dead.html

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records of both HQ and all field offices for all relevant names, agencies, organizations, companies and events including but not limited to those cited in the previous paragraphs and/or links as well as a cross-reference with the Southern Poverty Law Center to include any information provided by the SPLC. My request includes but is not limited to 137, 157, 176, 177, 183, 184, 188, and 214 files. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

If any part of these documents are exempt from disclosure, please be sure to review and release all reasonably segregable nonexempt portions of responsive documents, as the FOIA requires.

I request that I be properly classified as a media requester. I am an assistant editor at Reason Magazine who has written numerous articles on topics of public interest, and I am requesting these documents in an editorial capacity. Sincerely,

Matthew Petti

title: NCAA Financial Report (Virginia Commonwealth University)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

To Whom It May Concern,

I request the NCAA Membership Financial Reporting System revenue and expense reports for fiscal (or reporting) years 2004, 2005, 2006, 2007, 2008, 2009 & 2024.  Specifically:  

These reports are completed by the athletic department for the National Collegiate Athletic Association for the fiscal years listed above. This report is a multi-page document that had to be submitted to the NCAA in January. It contains 30+ revenue and expense categories, followed by specific breakdowns of each of those categories, by sport and gender. I am requesting the full report, including the detail tables, the “Other Reporting Items” list and the “Statement of Revenues and Expenses” that appears at the end of the report. PLEASE NOTE: The NCAA report is different than the equity report that is sent to the U.S. Department of Education for Title IX compliance.

If your department does not maintain these public records, please let me know who does and include the proper custodian’s name and address.

I prefer to receive the records electronically as an Adobe Acrobat (.pdf) file.

If you choose to deny this request, please provide a written explanation for the denial including a reference to the specific statutory exemption(s) on which you rely.  Also, please provide all segregable portions of otherwise exempt material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Stacey Brook.

title: Deaths in custody of Massachusetts county jail sheriff's departments

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Deaths in custody of Massachusetts county jail sheriff's departments
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Records sufficient to show the number of deaths of individuals while in custody, broken down by each Massachusetts county sheriff’s department (i.e., each county jail facility), from January 1, 2020 to the present.

For each death, I request:

1. The county sheriff’s office or facility where the death occurred
2. The date of death
3. The manner of death (e.g., natural causes, suicide, homicide, accidental, or undetermined), if available

If the requested information exists in a summary, spreadsheet, or annual report format, I request those records. If not, I request any records sufficient to reasonably compile this information.

If your office does not maintain these records, I request that you identify the agency or agencies that do, or forward this request as appropriate.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.
